audit_items.proof_trace: Neither ldap_bind() nor ldap_bind_s() contains an assert or null check for the `ld` parameter before dereferencing ld->ld_errno in the default/SASL branch (L79 and L115 respectively). Contrast with ldap_simple_bind() in sbind.c which guards with assert(ld != NULL) at L70. Proof fails: if ld is NULL and authmethod is not LDAP_AUTH_SIMPLE, the dereference is unconditional and will crash.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An application that fails to check the return value of ldap_init() (which returns NULL on allocation failure) and then calls ldap_bind(NULL, dn, passwd, LDAP_AUTH_SASL) or an unknown authmethod will trigger a null pointer dereference crash; an attacker able to exhaust memory or influence authmethod selection at the application level can trigger a denial-of-service.
